commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=fbd794d9676c7bb9490226b768d1fbfe5251e80f)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes Matrix crypto bootstrap sequencing and tests so password-backed forced reset bootstraps SSSS before cross-signing, removes the post-SSSS second cross-signing call, and preserves passwordless deferral.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +4. Total +6 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: with `getPassword` absent and a repairable SSSS error such as `getSecretStorageKey callback returned falsey`, the PR head still forwards the repair allowance and reaches the branch that recreates SSSS and calls `setupNewCrossSigning` again. I did not run a live Matrix homeserver reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review remains because the branch needs a code fix for the passwordless stale-SSSS path plus contributor or maintainer live Matrix proof; automation cannot supply the missing real-environment proof for this external PR.

target.title: fix(matrix): prevent double bootstrapCrossSigning reset in --force-reset-cross-signing
target.body:
## Summary

- `--force-reset-cross-signing` calls `bootstrapCrossSigning` twice: once with `setupNewCrossSigning: true` in the forced-reset path, then again via a redundant deferred-repair block that can trigger a second reset, destroying E2EE state
- Root cause: `MatrixCryptoBootstrapper.bootstrap()` deferred SSSS bootstrap for forced reset, ran a second `bootstrapCrossSigning` call after SSSS, and the repair block inside `bootstrapCrossSigning` could fire yet another `resetCrossSigning`
- This PR eliminates the double reset by:
  - For forced reset **with** a password: bootstrap SSSS upfront (non-strict, recreation allowed) before cross-signing, so broken SSSS is repaired before the destructive reset
  - For forced reset **without** a password (passwordless bots): preserve the deferral guard to avoid mutating SSSS before UIA succeeds
  - Disable the SSSS repair block inside `bootstrapCrossSigning` when upfront SSSS was done, preventing retrigger reset
  - Completely remove the second `bootstrapCrossSigning` call from the `bootstrap` method
- Fixes #78396

## Verification

- `pnpm test extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/crypto-bootstrap.test.ts` — 21 passed
- `pnpm test extensions/matrix` — 1390 passed across 121 files
- Fixed ESLint `no-implicit-coercion` on line 55 (`!!` → `Boolean()`) to resolve CI lint failures
- Tests confirm: no SSSS mutation for passwordless forced reset before UIA, single cross-signing reset, SSSS bootstrapped twice (before + after) for password holders
- Autoreview: pending
- CI: lint and bundled check failures fixed; waiting for fresh CI run on amended commit fbd794d

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Forced cross-signing reset no longer fires bootstrapCrossSigning twice; passwordless bots preserve the SSSS mutation guard
Real environment tested: Unit tests with mocked Matrix SDK; no live Matrix homeserver run
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test extensions/matrix` — all 1390 tests pass
Evidence after fix: Terminal output from running the crypto-bootstrap test showing 21 tests pass on the fixed code:
```console
$ pnpm test extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/crypto-bootstrap.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/user/codes/OPENSOURCES/openclaw

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  21 passed (21)
   Start at  00:21:40
   Duration  4.67s
```
Observed result after fix: Forced reset with password: SSSS upfront → single cross-signing reset → SSSS post-pass. Forced reset without password: no upfront SSSS → single cross-signing attempt → UIA failure with clean SSSS state
What was not tested: Live Matrix homeserver E2EE reset/recovery; Matrix SDK unit coverage only
